rationale: All homes in this Cape Coral, FL neighborhood display classic hip roof geometry with no dormers, steep gables, or architectural peaks. Shadow lengths cast from the eaves measure approximately 4–7 feet against a ~45-foot building width (using the 50 ft scale bar), consistent with a shallow pitch. Viewed from directly above, the roof surfaces appear very broad relative to their footprints with centrally positioned, low-prominence ridges — a strong indicator of 4:12 geometry. Regional norms for SW Florida strongly reinforce this estimate: hip roofs at 4:12 are the dominant residential pitch in Cape Coral due to hurricane wind-load code requirements and tile roofing minimums.
